justification: All six pass criteria met: (1) Three distinct integration patterns identified and designed correctly — SAP as batch/SFTP/XML with polling consumer, DHL as REST/webhook with async queue decoupling, Kafka as streaming consumer. (2) DHL 500ms webhook constraint explicitly addressed with a dedicated thin ingress that enqueues and returns 200, processing happens asynchronously. (3) DHL webhook idempotency addressed via messageId dedup store plus aggregate-level duplicate transition rejection. (4) SAP silent failure risk identified — file reprocessing after failure, dedup store with processing/completed states, crash recovery handling. (5) Dedicated integration layer recommended via ACL pattern with clear boundary separation from domain services. (6) Flexibility points explicitly cover adding new integrations via new adapters with zero changes to core domain.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough architecture — goes well beyond minimum requirements with outbox pattern, dead letter queues, context maps, and vertical slice implementation strategy
Clear separation of webhook ingress from DHL ACL processing with explicit rationale for the 500ms SLA
Dual-layer idempotency for DHL: dedup store at ACL level AND aggregate rejection of duplicate state transitions
SAP silent failure risk addressed with file hash tracking, processing states, and crash recovery
Explicit pitfalls-to-avoid section prevents common implementation mistakes
Correct decision to keep Kafka as downstream consumer only, not as the internal event bus
Data transformation layer cleanly separated via dedicated mappers (SapXmlMapper, DhlPayloadMapper, KafkaSchemaMapper) rather than embedding in services

agent_output_excerpt:
## Executive Summary

The integration architecture uses an **internal event bus as the canonical backbone**, with dedicated **Anti-Corruption Layers (ACLs)** for each external system. Each integration adapter translates between the internal domain model and external system protocols/formats independently, ensuring no external system's model pollutes the core domain. The design optimizes for independent deployability, failure isolation, and the vastly different temporal characteristics of each in
